Bli med på marsj for palestinske fanger 18. april kl. 14:30. Dette er ikke bare en markering – det er et rop om rettferdighet.

📍 Oslo S (Tigeren) → Nobel Fredssenter
🕘 Oppmøte 14:30

Over 10 000 palestinere sitter i israelske fengsler.
Blant dem er flere hundre barn og kvinner, mens flertallet er menn. Mange holdes uten tiltale eller rettssak. Familier venter, uten svar.

Bak hvert tall finnes et menneske. Et liv satt på pause. En stemme forsøkt stilnet.

Nå åpnes det også for bruk av dødsstraff.
Vi sier tydelig: Dødsstraff er uakseptabelt. Alltid. Ingen rettferdighet kan bygges på å ta liv.

Regjeringen uttrykker bekymring.
Vi sier: TOMME ORD REDDER IKKE LIV.
DET TRENGES HANDLING – SANKSJONER ISRAEL NÅ.

Stå sammen med oss. Vis at stillhet ikke er et alternativ.

Hvis du vil være med som "gissel" meld deg på via skjema som er lenket i bio hos @aksjonsgruppa.for.Palestina og delt i akajonskanalen 

Program ved Nobel fredssenter : Vi står 10 min stillhet i solidaritet med palestiske fanger .

Arrangør: Aksjonsgruppa for Palestina

Vil din organisasjon tilslutte seg? Send oss en dm eller skriv til aksjonsgruppa@proton.me

Det er global aksjon 17-18. april og fredag 18/4 kl 1500 er det aksjon og demonstrasjon ved det nye regjeringskvartalet i regi av @palestinakomiteeninorge @palestinakomiteenioslo @dpnf_nor @fellesutvalgetforpalestina @kampanjenpim og @aksjonsgruppa.for.palestina

Worshippers Return to Al-Aqsa Mosque After 40 Days of Closure

For the first time in 40 days, worshippers were finally allowed to enter Al-Aqsa Mosque after a prolonged closure imposed by the Israeli occupation.

Scenes show Palestinians walking back into the mosque, reclaiming their right to pray in one of Islam’s holiest sites after weeks of restriction and denial.

A moment of resilience, faith, and return despite ongoing limitations and control.

لأول مرة منذ 40 يوماً، سُمح للمصلين بالدخول إلى المسجد الأقصى بعد إغلاق طويل فرضه الاحتلال الإسرائيلي.

مشاهد توثّق عودة الفلسطينيين إلى الأقصى، واستعادتهم لحقهم في الصلاة في أحد أقدس الأماكن الإسلامية بعد أسابيع من المنع والقيود.

لحظة تعبّر عن الصمود والإيمان والعودة، رغم استمرار التضييق والسيطرة

Vil du hjelpe det palestinske fotballaget tilbake til Norway Cup i 2026? Det kan du gjøre i dag ⚽🇵🇸

Her om dagen ringte Ahmad – daglig leder for laget – og fortalte at gutta drømmer om å komme tilbake til Norway Cup i 2026. Da visste jeg at jeg måtte dele dette med dere, mine kjære falafelvenner.

I fjor fikk vi gleden av å møte dem – og spandere litt mat etter kampene. I år vil vi gjøre enda mer. 

La oss få dem til Norge sammen.
La oss støtte drømmen deres om å spille fotball, leve som barn og skape minner for livet. 

Hver eneste krone teller – og gjør en stor forskjell ❤️

Thousands of people gathered in Istanbul early in the morning to show solidarity with Palestine. After performing dawn prayers at major mosques, large crowds marched together and filled the Galata Bridge. The historic Galata Tower was covered with a giant Palestinian keffiyeh as a symbol of support.
The demonstration, organized by civil society groups, aimed to call for an end to the violence in Palestine and drew participation from more than 400 organizations.

Many reacted with disgust to Aftenposten’s speculative advertising campaign last week, in which they reproduced Trump’s vision for Gaza on huge billboards across the city.

Aftenposten had the following (gone) explanation: “With the advertisement, we wanted to say that to understand what is happening in the world you should read Aftenposten”

But what the advertisement showed was that Aftenposten does NOT understand the world.

But on Saturday morning these appeared in Oslo’s streets! We couldn’t agree more with the message, and thank you very much! To whoever you are!!

Aftenposten also said that they “wanted to point to a debate” and a question that is high on the agenda internationally, and which they as a newspaper cover.

No Aftenposten, the repeated fascist tricks from the White House are not a debate. Genocide should not be a debate either. They constitute serious threats, attacks and abuses - against international law, human rights and thus against all of us.

Serious editor-controlled media can and should be 100% clear on that.

Palestine belongs to the Palestinians. Stop the occupation, displacement and genocide. Condemn Apartheid Israel. Condemn Trump’s USA. Free Palestine.

Aftenposten quickly announced that they would withdraw the “Gaza advertisement”. That’s good. At the weekend, however, they announced that they will pursue those who “abuse” their brand.

@eyadalzaro 📸

The streets of Raholt were filled with demonstrators holding banners and chanting messages of support for the Palestinian cause. The vibrant energy of the crowd reflected a deep sense of compassion and shared humanity, transcending borders and cultures.

The month of Ramadan, a time of reflection and compassion, provided the perfect backdrop for this show of unity. Norwegian citizens, regardless of their backgrounds, came together to advocate for peace and an end to the suffering in Palestine.

The powerful voices of the Norwegian people and all peoples resonated throughout the city, amplifying a message of hope and solidarity that will not be silenced. The demonstration was a testament to the enduring humanitarian spirit and the collective strength of people standing together for a just cause.

Dabke is the Palestinian traditional dance 🍉🇵🇸

This project is a tribute to a Gazan man who danced the Dabke and the Dance of Freedom, which is a dance for the indigenous citizens of the US and Canada. He did this dance during the Gazans’ protests against the Israeli occupation while he and his friends were in Israelis’ sniper fire range back in 2018. His moves tell the world that we Palestinians shall know nothing but bravery! 

Hence, we wanted this project to pay tribute to this brave man. In our video, a Palestinian performer, @ghaliajum3aaa made the exact moves wearing an outfit similar to that of the Gazan hero; his identity remains unknown until now. 

What’s the origin of the Dabke in Palestine? There are many theories; one is that people in villages would stomp on the rooftops of houses together to compress the material to prevent cracks from forming. This eventually evolved into the dancing form of Dabke that is known today. Dabke is an exuberant, joyful form of traditional line and circle dancing originating from Middle Eastern culture and Bedouin tribes. Large groups interlock arms, tap and stomp with their feet, and sing or chant. Their purpose is to share historical narratives and vibrant folk legacies. We perform Dabke at our weddings, cultural events, and protests 🙌🏻

We aim to shed light on the art and culture of Palestine to humanize the storytelling narrative about Palestinians as news outlets tell our stories as if we are soulless numbers.

Project credit:
Director, producer, DOP, postproduction:@moamenghanim
Dabke performer: @ghaliajum3aaa
Stylist: @ayatallaX
BTS videographer: @reginahossfeldt

@zara a Fast Fashion retailer that has its share of human rights and environmental violations, decided it would be a good idea to utilize genocidal imagery: a body wrapped in a white cloth, a destroyed infrastructure that resembles a home, a white and blond haired human holding the dead body of a human wrapped in a white cloth-missing from this creative direction would be the Arabic letters written in sharpy over the white cloth to identify the human whose life was violently taken by the military occupation stealing land and murdering Indigenous peoples for finite resources used to further exacerbate our fragile ecosystem pushing us closer to global climate collapse. But how is Fashion related to politics, you ask?
Welcome to my body of work on Fashion & Colonialism, please refer to my Open Edu series, hundreds of hours of free accessible cultural theory and information on how the two intertwined reinforce the colonial capitalist empires that dominate our economy and world.
I am here to make a point however, when @zara uses these images to sell you a polyester (fossil-fuel based material) emitting more microplastics into our ecosystem and polluting our water, it is doing so not by accident but strategically knowing folks like myself would share it, would protest against it further extending its reach and platforming their mess on my platform. I just wanted to provide context for the ones looking to understand how Fashion plays a key role in genocide.
Consider this a developing project—the silence of fashion outlets is a direct endorsement of the ongoing genocides happening in real time: Palestine, Congo, Sudan and the many human rights violations they are complicit of.
Fashion has gone back to its colonial culture of ignoring reality, silencing political and intellectual discourse and further normalizing atrocities that endanger our collective lives as “fashion”.
Fashion continues to ignore voices like mine. Continues to platform people who distract the masses into purchasing instead of mobilizing and promoting idea that being silent in the face of horror is more valuable than speaking out. So please: speak up!
